note: Maps the GOV.UK Organisations API 'format' string to the canonical body_type (vocab/body_type.json v0.2). Rewritten after the full ingest (2026-07-05): every 'format' value actually present in the 1255-org dataset is mapped, so nothing falls through silently. v0.1 mis-keyed 'Tribunal' (was 'Tribunal non-departmental public body') and 'Devolved government' (was 'Devolved administration'), and omitted five live formats — all fixed here. 'royal_charter_body' has no source 'format' (chartered bodies are outside this API) and is populated by a separate Privy Council tranche, not this map.
rule: Any 'format' not present in 'map' below -> body_type 'other_body', and the record is flagged for review (AGENT.md rule 3: no uncontrolled vocabulary drift; never drop). Formats listed in 'flag_for_review' are mapped but coarse/coerced and should also carry a classification-review marker for a human pass.

flag_notes.Sub organisation: Heterogeneous tail — some are true divisions/directorates, some near-agencies. Confirm on spot-check.
flag_notes.Court: Constitutionally distinct from tribunals; parked in other_body pending a possible dedicated 'court' class.
flag_notes.Special health authority: Could be executive_ndpb; left other_body until confirmed against sponsor/legislation.
flag_notes.Devolved government: Devolved internal machinery is Spiral 5; other_body for now.
flag_notes.Executive office: Single body; confirm identity before a firmer class.
